content.Text:
[Wed 2026-04-22 11:39 PDT] Create a new file `docs/community-evolution.html` - a self-contained interactive Graph Community Evolution Tracker for the GraphVisual project.

## What it does
An interactive tool that lets users simulate how graph communities evolve over time. Users can step through time snapshots as edges are added/removed, and the tool autonomously detects community events: births, deaths, merges, splits, growth, and shrinkage.

## Technical requirements
- Single self-contained HTML file (all CSS/JS inline)
- Uses Canvas for graph visualization
- Dark theme matching other tools in docs/ (background #1a1a2e or similar dark scheme)
- Responsive design

## Features to implement

### 1. Graph Setup
- Start with a random graph (default 30 nodes, 45 edges)
- 4 presets: "City Districts" (dense clusters), "Social Network" (power-law), "Supply Chain" (tree-like with cross-links), "Random Evolution"
- Text input for custom edge lists

### 2. Community Detection
- Implement Louvain-like modularity-based community detection (simplified greedy modularity optimization)
- Color-code nodes by community
- Show modularity score

### 3. Time Evolution
- Timeline slider with play/pause/step controls
- At each timestep, randomly add/remove edges (configurable rates)
- Users can also manually add/remove edges by clicking

### 4. Event Detection (the agentic part)
- Automatically detect and announce community events:
  - **Birth**: new community appears (group of nodes splits off or forms from isolated nodes)
  - **Death**: community dissolves (members scatter to other communities)
  - **Merge**: two communities combine into one
  - **Split**: one community divides into two+
  - **Growth**: community gains 30%+ members
  - **Shrinkage**: community loses 30%+ members
- Event log panel showing detected events with timestamps
- Visual highlights when events occur (flash affected nodes)

### 5. Analytics Dashboard
- Community count over time (line chart on Canvas)
- Modularity score over time
- Largest community size over time
- Event frequency bar chart

### 6. Proactive Insights
- "Fragile Community" warnings (communities with weak internal connections likely to split)
- "Merger Candidates" (communities with many cross-edges)
- "Isolated Drift" alerts (nodes drifting away from their community)

## Layout
- Top: title bar with preset buttons
- Left (60%): Canvas graph visualization with force-directed layout
- Right top: Controls (play/pause/step, speed, edge add/remove rates)
- Right middle: Event log (scrollable, newest first)
- Right bottom: Mini analytics charts
- Bottom bar: Timeline slider + proactive insight alerts

## Style guide (match existing tools)
- Look at the pattern from other HTML files in docs/ - they use dark backgrounds, rounded cards, subtle borders
- Use CSS variables for theming
- Smooth animations
- Unicode icons for events (🟢 birth, 💀 death, 🤝 merge, ✂️ split, 📈 growth, 📉 shrink)

## Also update docs/index.html
Add a link/card for community-evolution.html in the tools listing. Look at how other tools are listed and follow the same pattern. Add it in the appropriate section (likely near graph-evolution.html or communities.html).

After creating the file, verify it's valid HTML by checking the structure is complete (opening/closing tags match, no syntax errors in JS).
I’m inspecting the existing `docs/` HTML tools and `docs/index.html` so I can match the project’s dark UI pattern before I build the new tracker and wire it into the listing.

📊 GraphVisual Community evolution visualization for student social networks

🚀 Get Started 📖 API Reference 🍳Cookbook ⭐ GitHub

What is GraphVisual?

GraphVisual is a Java desktop application for studying community evolution in student populations using Bluetooth proximity data. It processes meeting records from a PostgreSQL database, classifies social relationships, and renders interactive graph visualizations with timeline playback.

Built for research on social network analysis — specifically understanding how communities form, dissolve, and evolve over time in university settings.

Features

🎨

Interactive Visualization

Drag, zoom, and rotate graph nodes using JUNG's graph mouse. Explore community structures with cluster-based layouts.

⏱️

Timeline Playback

Animate community graphs across 92 days with play/pause/stop controls. Watch relationships form and dissolve.

🏷️

5 Relationship Types

Friends, classmates, study groups, familiar strangers, and strangers — each color-coded on the graph.

🎛️

Adjustable Thresholds

Tune meeting duration and frequency thresholds per relationship type in real-time.

📊

Network Statistics

Real-time metrics: node/edge counts, density, degree stats, hub identification, centrality analysis.

🔬

Community Detection

Connected-component analysis with modularity scoring, community density, and dominant edge-type breakdowns.

🧭

Shortest Path Finder

BFS (hop-count) and Dijkstra (weight-optimal) path finding between any two nodes.

💾

GraphML Export

Export graphs to standard GraphML format for Gephi, Cytoscape, NetworkX, and other tools.

🦠

Diffusion Simulator

Simulate epidemic and information spreading on networks with SIR, SIS, and Independent Cascade models. Proactive containment recommendations.

🏝️

Independent Set Finder

Find maximum independent sets with greedy, exact backtracking, and complement-clique algorithms. Step-by-step animation with Gallai theorem verification.

Relationship Classification

TypeColorLocationDurationFrequency
FriendsGreenPublic areas> 10 min≥ 2/day
ClassmatesBlueClassrooms> 30 min≥ 1/day
Study GroupsOrangeClassrooms> 20 min≤ 1/day
Familiar StrangersGrayPublic/paths< 2 min> 1/day
StrangersRedPublic/paths< 2 min< 2/day

All thresholds are adjustable at runtime via the Category Panel sliders.

Quick Start

# Clone
git clone https://github.com/sauravbhattacharya001/GraphVisual.git
cd GraphVisual

# Configure database
export DB_HOST=localhost DB_USER=your_user DB_PASS=your_pass

# Build with Maven
mvn initialize -P install-local-deps
mvn package -B

# Run
java -jar target/graphvisual-1.1.0-all.jar
See the full setup guide for detailed instructions including Docker, Ant builds, and data pipeline setup.

Tech Stack

TechnologyPurpose
Java 11+Application language
JUNG 2.0.1Graph data structures and visualization
Java SwingDesktop GUI framework
PostgreSQLMeeting and Bluetooth event storage
MavenBuild and dependency management
JUnit 4Unit testing
GitHub ActionsCI/CD, CodeQL, Pages deployment

Documentation

🚀

Setup Guide

Installation, configuration, building, running the data pipeline, Docker deployment.
🏗️

Architecture

Project structure, data pipeline, algorithm details, component design.
🗄️

Database Schema

PostgreSQL table schemas, query patterns, data pipeline ordering.
📖

API Reference

Detailed class and method documentation for all public APIs.
